models.note: DPO-aligned Mistral-7B fine-tune that popularized distilled preference tuning; weights, training recipe (alignment-handbook), and data all open under MIT.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real Oct 25 2023 Hugging Face H4 release, MIT license, Mistral-7B-v0.1 base (7.24B params is the well-established exact count for that base model). All 8 benchmark rows (MT-Bench 7.34, AlpacaEval 90.6, MMLU 61.07, HellaSwag 84.36, ARC-Challenge 62.03, GSM8K 12.74, TruthfulQA 57.45, Winogrande 77.74) verified verbatim on the official model card — no changes needed.

models.name: Idefics2 8B
models.archDisplay: SigLIP-SO400M vision encoder + Mistral-7B-v0.1 decoder, connected via learned Perceiver resampler + MLP modality projection
models.note: Compact Apache-2.0 VLM roughly 10x smaller than IDEFICS-80B, with strong OCR/document QA via SigLIP + Mistral-7B.
models.tpsNote: llama.cpp only converts the Mistral-7B LLM portion (mmproj vision tower unsupported), so no verified end-to-end Q4 4090 throughput exists.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real April 15 2024 Hugging Face release, Apache-2.0, 8B params (SigLIP-SO400M + Mistral-7B-v0.1), 32K/8K-sliding-window context inherited from Mistral-7B-v0.1 — all verified against the official blog. CORRECTED: the source blog publishes two parallel rows ('w/o image splitting' vs 'w/ image splitting'); the original record's MMMU (43.5) and MathVista (51.6) values actually belonged to the 'w/o image splitting' row while TextVQA/DocVQA/MMBench/VQAv2 belonged to the 'w/ image splitting' row — an inconsistent mix. Corrected MMMU to 43.0 and MathVista to 51.4 to match the 'with image splitting' row consistently with the other 4 (already-correct) rows, since that configuration is also the one that matches the note's claim of 'strong OCR/document QA' (74.0 DocVQA vs. 67.3 without splitting).

models.name: SmolLM2 1.7B
models.archDisplay: Llama-style decoder-only Transformer (SwiGLU, RoPE, tied embeddings), 24 layers, 2048 hidden dim
models.note: Fully-open small-model family (135M/360M/1.7B) for on-device use, trained on 11T curated tokens (FineWeb-Edu, DCLM, Stack, custom math/code).
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real 2024-11-01 Hugging Face release, Apache-2.0, 1.7B params, trained on 11T tokens — verified against the official model card and independent coverage (Simon Willison, MarkTechPost). All 4 base-model benchmark rows (HellaSwag 68.7, ARC avg 60.5, MMLU-Pro MCF 19.4, GSM8K 5-shot 31.0) confirmed verbatim on that card — no changes needed.

models.name: SmolLM3 3B (Think)
models.archDisplay: Decoder-only Transformer with GQA + NoPE (selective RoPE removal) + intra-document masking
models.note: Default extended-thinking mode (/think); open 3B multilingual dual-mode reasoner with 128K context, trained on 11.2T tokens.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real 2025-07-08 Hugging Face release, Apache-2.0, 3.08B params, GQA+NoPE decoder trained on 11.2T tokens with 64K native / 128K YaRN-extended context — all verified against the official HF blog. Both benchmark rows (AIME 2025 reasoning-mode 36.7%, LiveCodeBench reasoning-mode 30.0%) confirmed verbatim on that same blog — no changes needed.

models.name: SantaCoder 1.1B
models.archDisplay: GPT-2-style decoder with multi-query attention (GPTBigCode) + fill-in-the-middle
models.note: BigCode's first model: 1.1B multilingual (Python/Java/JS) code LLM with FIM infilling, proof-of-concept for The Stack.
models.verificationNotes: Fetched HF model card: confirmed 1.1B params, 2048-ctx, BigCode OpenRAIL-M, HumanEval 18.0/MBPP 35.0 pass@1 verbatim. arXiv 2301.03988 confirms project timeline; independent web search corroborates Dec 22 2022 release date. No changes.

models.name: StarCoder (15.5B)
models.archDisplay: GPT-2-style decoder with multi-query attention (GPTBigCode) + fill-in-the-middle, 8K context
models.note: Pivotal 15.5B open code LLM trained on The Stack across 80+ languages; matched Codex-cushman on HumanEval/MBPP.
models.verificationNotes: Fetched HF blog (dated 2023-05-04) and model card: confirmed ~15B params, 80+ languages, BigCode OpenRAIL-M, HumanEval 33.6/MBPP 52.7 verbatim. No changes.

models.name: StarCoder2 15B
models.archDisplay: Transformer decoder, grouped-query + sliding-window attention (4K window/16K ctx), FIM; NVIDIA-trained
models.note: Flagship 2nd-gen BigCode model, NVIDIA-trained on The Stack v2 (600+ languages, 4T+ tokens); HF/ServiceNow/NVIDIA collaboration.
models.verificationNotes: Fetched HF blog (2024-02-28) and model card: confirmed 15B params, 16K ctx/4K sliding window, NVIDIA-trained (NeMo+Eos), BigCode OpenRAIL-M, HumanEval 46.3/GSM8K(PAL) 65.1/DS-1000 33.8 verbatim. No changes.

models.name: StarCoder2 3B
models.archDisplay: Transformer decoder, grouped-query + sliding-window attention (4K window/16K ctx), FIM; ServiceNow-trained
models.note: ServiceNow-trained 3B StarCoder2 for laptop/edge code generation; outperforms original 15B StarCoder on many benchmarks.
models.verificationNotes: Fetched HF model card: confirmed 3B params, 16K ctx, ServiceNow-trained, 17 languages, HumanEval 31.7/GSM8K(PAL) 27.7/DS-1000 25.0 verbatim. No changes.

models.name: Skywork-13B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, 52 layers (thinner/deeper than Llama-2-13B), 4608 hidden dim
models.note: Bilingual (Chinese/English) base LLM trained on 3.2T tokens; best-in-class among ~13B Chinese open models at release
models.verificationNotes: Fetched HF model card: confirmed 13B params, 4K ctx, Skywork Community License, 3.2T tokens bilingual, MMLU 62.1/C-Eval 60.6/CMMLU 61.8/GSM8K 55.8 verbatim, Oct 2023 release. No changes.

models.name: Skywork-MoE-Base
models.archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 16 experts, 22B active (upcycled from Skywork-13B dense checkpoints)
models.note: 146B/22B-active MoE upcycled from Skywork-13B; first big open MoE runnable on a single 8x4090 server via FP8
models.tpsNote: Official FP8 checkpoint reaches ~2,200 tok/s aggregate across an 8x RTX4090 cluster via non-uniform tensor parallelism -- not a single-GPU llama.cpp Q4 measurement
models.verificationNotes: Fetched HF model card (146B total/22B active/16 experts, released 2024-06-03) and config.json (max_position_embeddings=8192, confirms ctxK:8) and arXiv 2406.06563v1 Table 1 (C-Eval 82.2, CMMLU 79.5, MMLU 77.4, GSM8K 76.1, MATH 31.9, HumanEval 43.9, all verbatim). No changes.

models.name: Skywork-o1-Open-Llama-3.1-8B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (Llama-3.1-8B architecture), fine-tuned with o1-style slow-thinking + process-reward-model data
models.note: Early open o1-style reasoning model built on Llama-3.1-8B with PRM-guided slow-thinking reasoning traces
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real HF repo exists with initial commit dated 2024-11-26 (matches release date). Config.json confirms max_position_embeddings=131072 (128K ctx, via RoPE scaling from base 8192) and 8B Llama-3.1 architecture. License and Kunlun/Skywork attribution confirmed via HF model card and press coverage of Skywork o1 series. No results rows were present to verify (none claimed).

models.name: Skywork-R1V (38B)
models.archDisplay: InternViT-6B-448px-V2_5 vision encoder + DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B LLM via lightweight visual projector
models.note: First open-sourced multimodal reasoning model with visual chain-of-thought, pairing InternViT-6B with DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: arxiv 2504.05599 and HF/GitHub repos exist. Fetched arxiv 2504.05599 (html) directly and confirmed Table 1 literally shows MMMU 69.0, MathVista 67.5, MATH-500 94.0, AIME 72.0, GPQA 61.6 for Skywork-R1V-38B — all 5 result rows confirmed verbatim. Architecture (InternViT-6B-448px-V2_5 + DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B) confirmed via HF model card. Release date 2025-03-18 and MIT license confirmed via press/GitHub.

models.name: CodeGen 16B (Mono)
models.archDisplay: Dense autoregressive transformer (GPT-2-style), 16.1B params, trained on TPU-v4
models.note: Early open program-synthesis LLM (350M-16B) trained on TPU-v4; Mono variant was competitive with OpenAI Codex on HumanEval
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: arxiv 2203.13474 (v1 submitted 2022-03-25) and HF repo Salesforce/codegen-16B-mono exist. Fetched ar5iv paper directly and confirmed Table 1 literally shows CodeGen-Mono 16.1B pass@1 = 29.28 on HumanEval. License BSD-3-Clause confirmed via HF/search.

models.name: CodeT5+ 16B
models.archDisplay: Encoder-decoder (shallow encoder / deep decoder), initialized from CodeGen-350M-mono encoder + CodeGen-16B-mono decoder
models.note: Encoder-decoder code LLM family (220M-16B); InstructCodeT5+ 16B tuned variant hit open-source SOTA HumanEval pass@1 in 2023 (non-commercial license)
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: arxiv 2305.07922 (submitted 2023-05-13) and HF repo Salesforce/codet5p-16b exist. Fetched ar5iv paper directly and confirmed base CodeT5+ 16B scores exactly 30.9 pass@1 / 51.6 pass@10 on HumanEval (distinct from the higher-scoring InstructCodeT5+ 16B variant at 35.0/54.5, which the record correctly does not conflate). License BSD-3-Clause confirmed on HF.

models.name: XGen-7B 8K
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer, trained with staged 2K to 4K to 8K sequence-length curriculum, OpenAI Tiktoken tokenizer
models.note: General-purpose 7B LLM trained on 1.5T tokens with an 8K context window under Apache-2.0; Salesforce's long-context entry
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: arxiv 2309.03450 and HF repo Salesforce/xgen-7b-8k-base exist; Salesforce blog, Forbes, MarkTechPost all confirm late-June/July 2023 announcement (release date 2023-07-03 retained). Fetched ar5iv paper directly and confirmed Table 6 literally shows MMLU 32.1, HellaSwag 74.2, ARC-Challenge 41.2, Winogrande 64.9 — all 4 result rows confirmed verbatim. License Apache-2.0 confirmed.

models.name: CodeGen2.5 7B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (mono/multi variants; instruct variant is research-purposes-only)
models.archDisplay: Dense autoregressive transformer decoder (GPT-style, causal + infill objective)
models.note: 7B code model matching 15B-16B peers (StarCoder-15B, CodeGen1/2-16B) via infill sampling and Flash-attention serving.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Salesforce blog post 'CodeGen2.5: Small, but mighty' published 2023-07-06, and HF repos codegen25-7b-mono_P/multi_P/instruct_P exist. Fetched CodeGeeX2 README directly and confirmed it literally lists CodeGen2.5-7B-mono pass@1 = 33.4 (also pass@10 58.4, pass@100 82.7). Config.json confirms max_position_embeddings=2048 (2K ctx) and ~7B-scale Llama-like dense decoder architecture. License split (Apache-2.0 mono/multi, research-only instruct) confirmed via search of HF model cards.

models.name: BLOOM (176B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer (ALiBi positional encoding, GPT-2-style, GeLU)
models.note: First openly-released 100B+ LLM, built by 1000+ BigScience researchers across 46 natural + 13 programming languages under RAIL license.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed on HF model card: 176,247,271,424 params (176.25B), RAIL v1.0 license, 2048-token context, 46 natural + 13 programming languages (59 total), HumanEval pass@1 15.5% all literally present on the page. July 11, 2022 release date matches the well-documented coordinated BLOOM family release. No changes made.

models.name: BLOOM-7B1
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer (ALiBi positional encoding, GPT-2-style, GeLU)
models.note: 7.07B multilingual BLOOM checkpoint, the practical size for downstream fine-tuning and local deployment.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed on HF model card: 7,069,016,064 params (7.069B) exact match; RAIL v1.0 license; 2048-token context; 45 natural + 12 programming = 57 languages matches langCount exactly. Page states training ended ~July 5, 2022, consistent with the July 11, 2022 public release date shared across the BLOOM checkpoint family. No changes made.

models.name: BLOOMZ (176B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer (BLOOM architecture), instruction fine-tuned on xP3
models.note: BLOOM 176B fine-tuned on the xP3 crosslingual task mixture for zero-shot multilingual instruction following.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed on HF model card: 176B params, RAIL v1.0 license, Winogrande XL 59.27, XWinograd-English 69.08, ANLI-r1 48.6 all literally present. arXiv 2211.01786 (Crosslingual Generalization through Multitask Finetuning) was submitted November 3, 2022, exactly matching the record's release date. No changes made.

models.name: OpenHermes 2.5 Mistral 7B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer decoder (Mistral architecture: GQA + sliding-window attention)
models.note: Hugely influential Mistral-7B fine-tune on ~1M mostly GPT-4-generated samples; adding code data lifted HumanEval from 43% to 50.7% pass@1.

results.notes: BigBench average (custom subset used by Nous/Teknium)

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed on HF model card: Apache-2.0 license, HumanEval 50.7 pass@1, TruthfulQA MC2 53.04, AGIEval avg 43.07, BigBench avg 40.96 all literally present. CORRECTED releaseDate from 2023-11-01 to 2023-11-03: multiple independent sources (aimodels.fyi, llm-explorer, general web search consensus) consistently cite November 3, 2023 as the release date, not November 1.

models.name: Nous Hermes 2 Mixtral 8x7B DPO
models.archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts transformer (8 experts, top-2 routing, Mixtral architecture)
models.note: Flagship MoE fine-tune of Mixtral 8x7B, SFT+DPO on 1M+ mostly GPT-4-generated samples; first community model to beat Mixtral Instruct broadly.

results.notes: BigBench average (custom subset used by Nous)

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed on HF model card: Apache-2.0 license, ARC-C norm 64.25, HellaSwag norm 84.89, Winogrande 76.16, AGIEval 46.05, BigBench 49.70 all literally present. Mixtral base spec (46.7B total / 12.9B active / 32K context) confirmed verbatim on Mistral AI's official 'Mixtral of experts' blog post. Decoded the cited tweet's Twitter snowflake ID (1746988416779309143) to 2024-01-15 20:11 UTC, exactly matching the record's release date, and confirmed via search that the tweet text matches the quoted 'first model to beat Mixtral Instruct' claim. No changes made.

models.name: Hermes 3 405B
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer decoder (Llama 3.1 architecture, grouped-query attention)
models.note: First full-parameter fine-tune of Llama 3.1 405B; Nous's neutrally-aligned flagship generalist with strong agentic, function-calling and roleplay ability.
models.verificationNotes: Fetched and read the full arXiv 2408.11857 PDF (Hermes 3 Technical Report, arXiv watermark dated 15 Aug 2024, exactly matching record's release date). Table 5 'Final downstream task evaluations' literally shows all seven claimed Hermes-3-405B scores verbatim: MMLU 85.02 (5-shot), MMLU-PRO 54.14 (5-shot), GPQA 44.84 (0-shot), ARC-C 69.45 (0-shot), BBH 75.37 (3-shot), HellaSwag 90.19 (10-shot), TruthfulQA MC2 65.57 (0-shot). Paper confirms Llama 3.1 base sizes (8B/70B/405B) and 131,072 (128K) native context window. HF model card lists 406B raw param count vs. the record's standard '405B' naming; kept 405 as this is the universally used name for this Llama 3.1 size class. No changes made.

models.name: Guanaco 33B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (LoRA adapter); base LLaMA-33B weights under Meta's non-commercial research license
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer (LLaMA-33B base) with 4-bit QLoRA adapter finetune
models.note: QLoRA 4-bit finetune of LLaMA-33B on OASST1 that reached 97.8% of ChatGPT's Vicuna-benchmark quality (second-best in the paper, after the 65B model's 99.3%) after roughly 12 hours on a single 24GB consumer GPU.

results.notes: Elo rating, Vicuna benchmark, human raters, rank 4
Elo rating, OpenAssistant benchmark, GPT-4 judge, rank 4

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model via HF model card (fetched). paramsB=33, license, ctxK=2 (LLaMA-1 base), MMLU=57, Vicuna Elo=1009 (human, rank4), OpenAssistant Elo=1002 (GPT-4 judge, rank4) all literally match the HF page. releaseDate 2023-05-23 confirmed via arXiv abstract page (2305.14314) which also literally states 'reaching 99.3% of the performance level of ChatGPT' but CORRECTED the note field: fetched full paper text (ar5iv) shows the 99.3% figure belongs to the 65B model; Guanaco 33B is explicitly called 'the second best model reaching 97.8% of the performance level of ChatGPT' and Table 6 lists 'Guanaco 33B ... 97.8%'. The 'trained on 24GB consumer GPU in <12 hours' claim for 33B is verbatim-confirmed in the paper. Original record incorrectly attributed 99.3% to the 33B model; fixed to 97.8%.

models.name: Guanaco 65B
models.license: Apache-2.0 (LoRA adapter weights); base LLaMA weights remain under Meta's non-commercial research license
models.archDisplay: LLaMA (dense decoder-only transformer) + 4-bit QLoRA adapter
models.note: 4-bit QLoRA fine-tune of LLaMA 65B on OASST1, reaching 99.3% of ChatGPT's performance on the Vicuna benchmark in ~24 GPU-hours on one 48GB GPU.
models.tpsNote: 65B does not fit in a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at Q4 (~39GB needed); no verified single-GPU llama.cpp measurement found.

results.notes: Vicuna benchmark Elo rating (human-judged); GPT-4-judged Elo was 1022. Paper separately reports reaching 99.3% of ChatGPT's performance level on this benchmark.

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model via HF model card (fetched): 65B params, Apache-2.0 adapter license, Vicuna Elo human=1023/GPT-4=1022 literally match the page. releaseDate 2023-05-23 confirmed via arXiv (2305.14314). The paper's full text confirms 99.3% and '24 hours on a single [48GB] professional GPU' specifically describe the 65B (largest/best) model, so this note is correct as-is (no change needed, unlike the 33B record).

models.name: Airoboros 70B (L2)
models.license: Llama 2 Community License Agreement (custom Meta license)
models.archDisplay: Llama 2 (dense decoder-only transformer)
models.note: Fully synthetic, GPT-4-generated self-instruct fine-tune of Llama 2 70B; a solo-developer pipeline that produced a broadly competitive open 70B chat model.
models.tpsNote: 70B does not fit in a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM at Q4 (~40GB needed); no verified single-GPU llama.cpp measurement found.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model via HF model card fetch (jondurbin/airoboros-l2-70b-gpt4-2.0): 70B Llama-2 finetune, custom Llama-2 license, explicit coding + function-calling dataset content confirmed. All four Open-LLM-Leaderboard CSV scores (MMLU 70.4, ARC 68.5, HellaSwag 87.9, TruthfulQA 49.8) fetched and literally confirmed on the cited CSV. releaseDate 2023-08-05 could not be pinned to the exact day (search shows the sibling TheBloke quantized repos were created late July/early August 2023, i.e. within days of the claimed date) — kept as-is since no contradicting date was found, but exact-day precision is unconfirmed.

models.name: Teuken-7B (instruct)
models.archDisplay: Custom decoder-only transformer with multilingual tokenizer, GQA, RMSNorm, SwiGLU, RoPE
models.note: EU-funded model pre-trained from scratch and instruction-tuned across all 24 official EU languages; Apache-2.0 commercial variant.
models.tpsNote: No RTX 4090-specific measurement found; a 7B model at Q4 fits comfortably and typically runs well above real-time on a 4090.

results.notes: EU21-MMLU, averaged across 21 EU languages (0-1 scale)

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model. Fraunhofer IAIS press release (fetched via search) confirms release date 2024-11-26 exactly and '24 official EU languages' / 7B params / open-source commercial license. HF README (fetched via raw.githubusercontent-style raw endpoint) confirms context length 4096 (=ctxK 4) and all four EU21 benchmark scores (MMLU 0.430, ARC 0.569, HellaSwag 0.620, TruthfulQA 0.503) literally match. No changes needed.

models.name: Sarashina2-70B
models.archDisplay: Llama2-style dense decoder-only transformer
models.note: MIT-licensed Japanese base model trained ground-up on ~2.1T tokens of Japanese, English, and code; not instruction-tuned.
models.tpsNote: 70B does not fit in a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM at Q4 (~40GB needed); no verified single-GPU measurement found.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real model. HF config.json (fetched) shows LlamaForCausalLM architecture, max_position_embeddings=8192 (=ctxK 8), hidden_size 8192, 80 layers — consistent with a 70B-class dense Llama-style model. HF commit history (fetched) shows the initial commit was made 2024-08-06, exactly matching the claimed releaseDate (a separate SB Intuitions tech-blog post about the model was dated 2024-08-21, but the HF repo's own commit history is the more precise/primary source and is used here). MIT license and 'not instruction-tuned' base-model status confirmed via search of SB Intuitions' own materials. 2.1T token / Japanese-English-code training mix confirmed via search results describing the Sarashina2 series (Japanese:English:Code ratio 5:4:1, ~2.1T tokens total). No results rows were present to check. No changes needed.
